data.title: Bitcoin institutional demand remains strong: Cryptoquant
data.description:
Cryptoquant found that large custody wallets accumulated $53 billion in Bitcoin over 12 months, suggesting that institutional demand for Bitcoin hasn’t gone away.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-institutional-demand-remains-strong-cryptoquant-ceo?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Trump Threatens 200% Tariff on French Wine as US–France Tensions Escalate
data.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to impose a 200% tariff on French wine, following reports that French President Emmanuel Macron rejected a proposed “Board of Peace” seat, intensifying diplomatic friction between Washington and Paris.

data.title: Binance Alpha to Launch HeyElsa (ELSA) Trading on January 20, Airdrop Details Announced
data.description: Binance Alpha will be the first platform to feature HeyElsa (ELSA), with Alpha trading scheduled to open on January 20, 2026, at 08:00 (UTC).Following the trading launch, eligible users will be able to participate in an ELSA token airdrop through the Binance Alpha Events page.ELSA Airdrop RulesEligibility: At least 235 Binance Alpha PointsAirdrop amount: 200 ELSA tokensDistribution method: First-come, first-servedDynamic threshold: If the reward pool is not fully claimed, the required Alpha Points will decrease by 5 points every 5 minutesCost to claim: 15 Binance Alpha PointsConfirmation window: Users must confirm their claim within 24 hours, or the reward will be forfeitedBinance advised users to follow its official channels for further updates regarding Alpha listings and event details.

data.title: Hong Kong securities industry groups oppose tightening rules on crypto asset management licenses.
data.description: The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Practitioners Association (HKSFPA), a Hong Kong securities industry association, submitted its comments to regulators regarding the proposed regulatory framework for digital asset management in Hong Kong, opposing several tightening measures. The HKSFPA primarily opposes the removal of the current "de minimis" arrangement. Under current rules, institutions holding a Type 9 license (asset management) can allocate no more than 10% of their total fund assets to crypto assets after reporting to regulators, without needing to apply for a separate virtual asset management license. The proposed scheme aims to remove this limit, requiring a full virtual asset management license even for a mere 1% allocation to Bitcoin. The HKSFPA argues that this "all or nothing" regulatory approach is disproportionate and could lead to significant compliance costs with limited risk exposure, thereby discouraging traditional asset management institutions from entering the crypto asset space. Furthermore, the HKSFPA also objects to the proposed custody requirements. The proposal mandates that virtual asset managers can only use SFC-licensed custodians for asset custody. The association stated that this requirement is impractical in early-stage token investment and Web3 venture capital, potentially limiting the scope of related business for Hong Kong-based institutions. Meanwhile, the HKSFPA expressed support for allowing self-custody and the use of qualified overseas custodians when serving professional investors. The report states that the aforementioned regulatory recommendations are already in the implementation phase. Hong Kong authorities had previously released a consultation summary on the relevant proposals and launched a new round of consultations on the licensing regime for crypto asset trading, advisory, and management services. (The Block)

data.title: Crypto Market Stabilizes as RWA Sector Leads Gains, NFT and SocialFi Lag
data.description: The cryptocurrency market showed signs of stabilization and recovery over the past 24 hours, with most sectors posting modest gains, according to data from SoSoValue.The real-world asset (RWA) sector outperformed the broader market, rising 2.38%, making it the strongest-performing category during the period.Within the RWA sector, Sky (SKY) gained 1.90%, Ondo Finance (ONDO) rose 2.74%, while Pendle (PENDLE) climbed 5.52%, leading sector momentum.Meanwhile, major cryptocurrencies traded within narrow ranges. Bitcoin (BTC) hovered near $92,000, while Ethereum (ETH) remained around $3,200, reflecting cautious market sentiment.Sector performance overviewSeveral other sectors recorded moderate gains:AI sector: +2.06%Kite (KITE): +7.79%Meme sector: +1.80%MemeCore (M): +3.61%PayFi sector: +1.30%Safe (SAFE): +4.27%Layer 1 sector: +0.97%Cosmos Hub (ATOM): +4.10%CeFi sector: +0.83%Binance Coin (BNB): +1.69%DeFi sector: +0.75%River (RIVER): +15.72%Layer 2 sector: +0.47%SOON (SOON): +10.83%NFT and SocialFi underperformIn contrast, two sectors closed in negative territory:NFT sector: −0.22%SocialFi sector: −1.61%The underperformance suggests continued weakness in speculative and engagement-based segments compared with yield- and utility-driven narratives.Sector indices show broad recoverySector indices tracking historical performance also reflected mild recovery:ssiMeme: +1.36%ssiRWA: +1.36%ssiAI: +1.05%Overall, the data indicates a cautiously improving market environment, with capital continuing to rotate toward RWA, AI, and infrastructure-related sectors, while NFT and SocialFi activity remains subdued.

data.title: South Korea's Democratic Party Develops Stablecoin Legislation
data.description: South Korea's Democratic Party is drafting a new bill focused on stablecoins, integrating essential aspects from five previous proposals. According to NS3.AI, the legislation seeks to define the entities authorized to issue stablecoins and regulate the ownership stakes of major shareholders in digital asset exchanges. The party plans to discuss this bill in conjunction with proposals from the opposition and government during an upcoming National Assembly committee meeting.

data.title: Peter Schiff Predicts Bitcoin Downturn Amid Silver Surge
data.description: Economist and gold advocate Peter Schiff has expressed concerns about Bitcoin's future. According to Foresight News, Schiff tweeted that the current trend in silver prices is likely to reverse and impact Bitcoin negatively. He warned that the rise in silver could signal a catastrophic collapse for Bitcoin, cautioning followers to heed his warning.

data.title: Binance Alpha: HeyElsa (ELSA) trading will begin on January 20th at 16:00 (UTC). The airdrop claim threshold is 235 points.
data.description: Binance Alpha announced that it will be the first platform to list HeyElsa (ELSA), with Alpha trading commencing on January 20, 2026 at 16:00 (UTC+8). The announcement states that after trading begins, users holding at least 235 Binance Alpha Points can claim an airdrop of 200 ELSA tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the event continues, the points threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha Points, and users must confirm within 24 hours on the Alpha event page; failure to do so will be considered a forfeiture of the claim.

data.title: Ethereum Network Activity Surge Linked to Address Poisoning Attacks: Researcher
data.description:
Part of Ethereum’s recent activity surge may be driven by address poisoning spam rather than organic user growth.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/ethereum-network-activity-surge-linked-to-address-poisoning-attacks-researcher/

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $231 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description: In the past 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market witnessed liquidations totaling $231 million, according to CoinAnk data. According to Foresight News, long positions accounted for $197 million of the liquidations, while short positions made up $34.04 million. Bitcoin saw liquidations of approximately $81.1 million, and Ethereum experienced liquidations of around $66.76 million.

data.title: Cardano's Price Shows Potential for Bullish Divergence
data.description: Cardano's price is exhibiting signs of a bullish divergence, reminiscent of a pattern that led to a 32% rally last year. According to NS3.AI, this development is bolstered by increased accumulation from whale wallets. While long-term holders remain inactive, which reduces downside risk, there is a rise in short-term activity that could lead to supply concerns. For a sustained uptrend to be confirmed, Cardano must surpass critical resistance levels at $0.41 and $0.43. Conversely, a decline below $0.35 would negate the bullish setup.

data.title: Federal Reserve Announces $8.3B Treasury Bill Purchase as Bitcoin Faces Market Volatility
data.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve has scheduled an $8.3 billion Treasury bill purchase for January 20, 2026, as part of a broader $55 billion liquidity injection into financial markets, according to data cited by NS3.AI.The announcement comes amid heightened volatility in the Bitcoin market, which has been pressured in recent sessions by geopolitical tensions and renewed tariff-related uncertainty.Liquidity injection amid macro instabilityThe Treasury bill purchase forms part of the Federal Reserve’s ongoing balance sheet operations, aimed at maintaining sufficient liquidity within the financial system. While the move does not represent a formal shift in monetary policy, it adds short-term liquidity at a time when global markets remain sensitive to political and macroeconomic developments.Bitcoin has experienced sharp price fluctuations in recent days, reacting to escalating trade tensions and broader risk-off sentiment across global markets.Bitcoin outlook remains cautiously optimisticDespite near-term pressure, some market participants maintain a cautiously optimistic outlook for Bitcoin, noting that liquidity injections have historically provided medium-term support for risk assets.However, analysts also warn that macroeconomic uncertainty remains elevated. Investor flows have recently favored traditional safe-haven assets such as gold and silver, both of which have reached record highs, potentially limiting Bitcoin’s upside in the short term.Short-term consolidation possibleWith tariff risks, geopolitical uncertainty, and shifting capital flows still dominating sentiment, Bitcoin may continue to consolidate or face further corrective moves before establishing a clearer trend.Market participants are closely monitoring whether sustained liquidity support and improving risk appetite can eventually translate into renewed momentum for digital assets.

data.title: Trader Utilizes High Leverage for Bitcoin Short Position
data.description:
On January 20, a trader entered a short position on Bitcoin using 40x leverage, according to BlockBeats. The position involved 130 BTC at an average entry price of $91,564.9, currently showing a slight profit.

The trader's address is known for employing high leverage strategies, demonstrating a keen ability to navigate short-term market fluctuations. The active management of funds highlights a sensitivity to market dynamics.

data.title: A new address withdrew 6,000 ETH from Bybit and deposited it into Aave, worth $19.03 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, in the past 3 hours, a newly created wallet 0x919c (0x919c...9B03) withdrew 6,000 ETH from Bybit, worth $19.03 million, and then deposited it into Aave.

data.title: MegaETH will launch its public mainnet on January 22 and begin global stress testing.
data.description: On January 20th, the Ethereum Layer 2 project MegaETH announced that its public mainnet will officially launch on January 22nd. The official statement indicated that several USDM-based DeFi applications will be launched simultaneously on the first day of the mainnet launch, along with a global stress test. The announcement revealed that the stress test plans to process approximately 11 billion transactions over 7 days, with a target throughput of 15,000–35,000 TPS during the test.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Surge in Address Poisoning Attacks Amid Lower Gas Fees
data.description: Ethereum has recently experienced a significant increase in transaction and address activity, potentially due to large-scale address poisoning attacks exploiting low gas fees. According to PANews, security researcher Andrey Sergeenkov highlighted that these attacks involve creating similar-looking addresses to conduct 'dust attacks,' misleading users into transferring funds to incorrect addresses. Following the Fusaka upgrade, transaction fees have dropped by over 60%, significantly reducing the cost of such attacks. Research indicates that more than 116,000 individuals have been affected, with cumulative losses exceeding $740,000.

data.title: Ethereum Community Center Set to Open in Hong Kong
data.description: The Ethereum ecosystem expansion team has announced the opening of the next Ethereum Community Center in Hong Kong on January 20. According to BlockBeats, the official launch event is scheduled for January 24, focusing on offline interactions, co-creation, and event hosting. Many users view the Hong Kong Community Center as a crucial node for connecting the Ethereum ecosystem in East Asia.

data.title: UK Committee Warns of AI Risks in Financial Services
data.description: A UK parliamentary committee has raised concerns about the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) in financial services, which is outpacing regulators' ability to manage associated risks. According to NS3.AI, the Treasury Committee criticized UK regulators for depending excessively on outdated regulations and called for clearer guidance on AI usage by 2026. Experts have pointed out that AI's complex and opaque systems pose challenges to traditional regulatory frameworks, potentially leading to harm and hindering responsible AI innovation.
